company_type: Technology - Cloud Computing and Cybersecurity Services

dependencies: Transmission capacity from third-party telecommunications network providers
Equipment including hundreds of thousands of servers deployed globally
Network bandwidth from third-party network providers
Server and networking equipment from manufacturers
risks: Disruptions in supply chain preventing purchase of needed equipment
Volatility in server component costs for compute platform build-out

Difficulty purchasing equipment manufactured in areas facing disruptions due to unrest, trade sanctions, political activity, public health issues, safety issues, natural disasters or general economic conditions
Tariffs imposed by United States on other countries and resulting counter-tariffs leading to increasing costs and supply chain disruptions
Inability to purchase bandwidth and co-location space due to limitations on provider resources or increasing energy costs
Dependence on third-party network providers and co-location facilities

regulatory_risks: Data privacy and localization laws (GDPR, CCPA)
Export controls and sanctions (ITAR, EAR, embargoed countries)
U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act and anti-bribery laws
Fair share or internet content taxes
Law enforcement access to information requirements
Restrictions on social media or content delivery (India restrictions on Chinese-owned applications, U.S. legislation prohibiting services to Chinese application)
Section 230 of U.S. Communications Decency Act potential repeal or amendment
Telecommunications regulations potentially applying to internet companies
OECD Pillar Two 15% global minimum corporate tax rate for large multinational enterprise groups
Industry regulations applicable to key suppliers to customers

sanction_risks: Sanctions imposed in connection with Russian invasion of Ukraine
U.S. government sanctions against embargoed countries and designated entities
Loss of revenues if U.S. or international governments impose limitations on doing business with significant customers
Restrictions on Chinese-owned applications (India 2020 restrictions, U.S. 2024 legislation targeting Chinese social media application)
Geopolitical pressures causing customers to reduce reliance on U.S. providers and implement DIY solutions
